full_text:
70 km wind, heavy rains, thunderstorm, heatwave alert in…: Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Faridabad, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Rajasthan, Himachal Pradesh to…, check full IMD forecast here
People in Delhi-NCR are currently struggling with rising temperatures, but there may be some relief in other parts of the country.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), a rain and thunderstorm alert has been issued for around 15 states, including Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Uttarakhand, on May 16. Residents have been advised to stay alert as strong winds and sudden weather changes are expected.
Delhi-NCR weather update
In Delhi, the weather will remain hot for now. The maximum temperature is likely to touch 39°C, while the minimum may stay around 28°C. However, in many other states, a shift in weather conditions could bring some drop in temperature due to rainfall and strong winds.
UP weather update
In Uttar Pradesh, the weather is expected to change by the evening, especially in Lucknow. IMD has issued alerts for districts like Kaushambi, Fatehpur, and Balrampur. Strong winds with speeds of 60–65 kmph and thunderstorms are likely. The temperature in Lucknow may reach a high of 40°C and a low of 28°C.
Uttarakhand update
Uttarakhand is also expected to see a major weather change. Heavy rainfall and storms have been predicted in areas such as Nainital, Bageshwar, Pithoragarh, Chamoli, Rudraprayag, Tehri Garhwal, and Udham Singh Nagar. Wind speeds could reach 60–70 kmph. In Dehradun, the maximum temperature is expected to be around 34°C, with a minimum of 22°C.
Rajasthan update
In Rajasthan, the day may begin with normal weather, but conditions are expected to change later. Cities like Alwar, Barmer, Dholpur, Jodhpur, Ajmer, Bharatpur, and Udaipur may witness rain and thunderstorms with wind speeds of 50–60 kmph. Jaipur may record a high of 41°C and a low of 29°C.
Himachal Pradesh update
Himachal Pradesh is on high alert for storms, especially in Kangra, Chamba, Mandi, Una, Bilaspur, Solan, and Shimla. Strong winds may blow at 70–80 kmph along with heavy rainfall.
